chapter_title.ar: باب لُحُومِ الْجَلَّالَاتِ وَ بَيْضِهِنَّ وَ الشَّاةِ تَشْرَبُ الْخَمْرَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 6 – Meats of the dung-eaters, and their eggs, and the sheep drinking the wine

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/44)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/44)
text: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ أَكْلِ لُحُومِ الدَّجَاجِ فِي الدَّسَاكِرِ وَ هُمْ لَا يَمْنَعُونَهَا مِنْ شَيْ‏ءٍ تَمُرُّ عَلَى الْعَذِرَةِ مُخَلًّى عَنْهَا وَ عَنْ أَكْلِ بَيْضِهِنَّ فَقَالَ لَا بَأْسَ بِهِ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Barqy, from Sa’ad Al Ashary,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Al-Hassan Al-Reza<sup>asws</sup>, said, ‘I asked him<sup>asws</sup> about eating the meat of the chicken in the villages, and they are not prevented from anything. So they pass upon the human excrement and are left alone upon it; and about their eggs. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘There is no problem with it’.
en.sarwar: 8. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from al-Barqiy from Sa‘d ibn Sa‘d al-Ash‘ariy who has said the following: “I once asked al-Rida’, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the chickens in a village which move around unrestricted. They may pass by feces freely and eat thereof and about their eggs. He (the Imam) said, ‘It is not unlawful.’”
